title: Fix MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline to Handle Nested Fields with Optional Keys

statement.inputFormat: A MongoDB collection `orders` with the specified document structure.
statement.outputFormat: The resulting document with the extracted `orderDetails` fields.
statement.notes: The pipeline should handle documents with missing `product` fields properly.

hints.text: Use the `$map` operator to iterate over the `orderDetails` array.
Use the `$mergeObjects` operator to merge the `product` and `quantity` fields.
Use the `$ifNull` operator to handle missing `product` fields.

editorial.title: Fixing Aggregation Pipeline to Handle Nested Fields with Optional Keys
editorial.intuition: The aggregation pipeline should use the `$map` operator to iterate over the `orderDetails` array and extract the `product` and `quantity` fields. Then, use the `$mergeObjects` operator to merge these fields into a single object. Finally, use the `$ifNull` operator to handle missing `product` fields by returning an empty array for those documents.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to use the `$ifNull` operator to check for missing `product` fields and then use the `$mergeObjects` operator to merge the fields. However, this approach can be inefficient and may not handle edge cases properly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses the `$map` operator to iterate over the `orderDetails` array and extract the `product` and `quantity` fields. Then, use the `$mergeObjects` operator to merge these fields into a single object. Finally, use the `$ifNull` operator to handle missing `product` fields by returning an empty array for those documents.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the aggregation pipeline correctly handles documents with missing `product` fields and returns the expected output.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run the pipeline, start by creating a sample document with missing `product` fields. Then, apply the pipeline to the document and verify that the output is correct. Finally, test the pipeline with a variety of input documents to ensure that it handles edge cases properly.
